parsed.rationale: The Caudal fin is located at the posterior end of the fish skeleton. Visually, the rays extending from the Caudal fin are significantly longer and more numerous than those of the other labeled fins. The Dorsal, Anal, and Pelvic fins all have shorter, more compact ray structures in comparison. Therefore, the Caudal fin possesses the longest fin rays.
